case_title: JENNIFER KAY TOY VS. ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: PERSONAL INJURY/PROPERTY DAMAGE - VEHICLE RELATED, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TOY, JENNIFER KAY AS TO DEFENDANT ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC. BROSNAN, JOHN DOES 1 TO 99 SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR APR-05-2017 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JAN-03-2017 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         MAR-13-2017
AMENDED PROOF OF SERVICE (TRANSACTION ID # 100010885) FILED BY DEFENDANT ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING HOW DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE HARMED HER. PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND CONSPIRACY ARE NOT DISTINCT CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS A CLAIM FOR DAMAGE. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD CONSPIRACY IN THE GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATIONS. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT CORRECTLY ARGUES THAT AN EMPLOYER CANNOT CONSPIRE WITH ITS EMPLOYEES. (SEE BLACK V. BANK OF AMERICA (1994) 30 CAL.APP.4TH 1, 4.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF APR-05-2017 CONTINUED TO JUN-14-2017 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT PLEAD A CLEAR BASIS FOR LIABILITY AGAINST DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING HOW DEFENDANT'S MALICIOUS, FRAUDULENT, OR OPPRESSIVE CONDUCT CAUSED PLAINTIFF HARM. (SEE BROUSSEAU V. JARRETT (1977) 73 CAL.APP.3D 864, 872.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Dismissal - No Appearance

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-28-2018 Set For Trial On Aug-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Continued To Apr-11-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jennifer Kay Toy 415-841-3081 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
David Armanini, Esq. 925-718-1040 of Norton & Melnik, APC for Defendants MATAGRANO, INC., JORGE REVILLEZA.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues opposing side was requesting for more time. Defendant argues Plaintiff cannot amend complaint. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS MATAGRANO, INC., JORGE REVILLEZA DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Matagrano, Inc. and Jorge Revilleza's demurrer to plaintiff Jennifer Kay Toy's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action. (C.C.P. 430.10(e).) Plaintiff does not sufficiently allege a breach of a duty. Paragraph 26 of the first amended complaint alleges that defendants' vehicle was delivering goods to a business and that such a vehicle can double park. (Veh. Code 22502(b)(1).) Plaintiff does not allege cognizable damages. "In California, the only kinds of damages caused by negligence which may be recovered from a defendant not in privity with plaintiff are for bodily injury and physical damage." (Anthony v. Kelsey-Hayes Co. (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 442, 446-447.) Here, plaintiff does not seek such damages. There can be no conspiracy claim without an underlying wrong.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

An Order is signed for the Demurrer and Motion to Strike. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AYSC)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Continued To Jun-14-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Allison L. Jones of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (619.696.6700) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing how defendants' alleged negligence harmed her. Punitive damages and conspiracy are not distinct causes of action. Plaintiff has leave to plead punitive damages as a claim for damage. Plaintiff also has leave to plead conspiracy in the general charging allegations. The court notes that defendant correctly argues that an employer cannot conspire with its employees. (See Black v. Bank of America (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 1, 4.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Allison L. Jones of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (619.696.6700) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV WORLDWIDE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with 20 days leave to amend. The complaint does not plead a clear basis for liability against defendant. Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing how defendant's malicious, fraudulent, or oppressive conduct caused plaintiff harm. (See Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)
